text:
Market today: Risk-off mood with a chip-specific gut-punch - Samsung and SK Hynix both crashed 7%+ today, signaling the semiconductor rout is spreading globally. That's the real driver for your book right now. Geopolitical noise (Russia strike, Poland jets) adds a risk-off undercurrent that historically punishes high-beta tech and levered ETFs hardest.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is bleeding - it's a 3X levered chip ETF getting hit from two directions: the underlying sector selling off AND daily decay. Stop is $200, watch it closely on margin. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, and already near its tight $21.38 stop - on margin, a fast move through that stop hurts real money. SMH (-2.5%) is the unleveraged version of your chip pain; less urgent but confirms the sector is under pressure.

Watch: Whether SMH holds above $540 stop level and whether the Samsung/Hynix selling bleeds into US chip names (TSM, AMD, NVDA) at the open.

Bottom line: Your leveraged chip exposure is the fire - manage SOXL and SPAL stops before looking at anything new.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - with -14.52% already in a 3X levered ETF on margin, decide NOW if you're holding that line or trimming before it's decided for you.
SPAL: Already -13.72% with stop at $21.38 - this is a brand-new volatile levered ETF, very close to your exit. One bad candle triggers it; be ready to honor the stop, no averaging down.
No new buys today - chip sector is actively breaking down globally; wait for the dust to settle before adding any new long exposure.
